content_description: Academic research presentation on a novel laparoscopic tool modification for needle reorientation in suturing procedures
key_observations: This is an educational/research presentation, not an FLS task performance video
Content includes: title slides, motivation charts, mechanical design schematics, prototype demonstrations, trajectory simulations, and user study results
No actual trainee performing an FLS standardized task with a trainer box

Presentation covers: needle reorientation problem (33% of novices require reorientation), modified needle driver design with rolling capability, biomechanical comparison, and user study data showing reduced time loss
video_classification_rationale: This video is academic/instructional content presenting research on a laparoscopic tool innovation. It contains:presentation slides with overlays, charts and graphs, mechanical design diagrams, simulation trajectories (CAD/3D rendered), and research data. No actual FLS task performance by a trainee or expert on a standard trainer is present.
cannot_score_reasons: No standardized FLS task trainer box visible
No actual task performance by a trainee or surgeon
Content is research presentation, not performance assessment
Video consists of slides, schematics, simulations, and data visualizations rather than real-time task execution

teaching_content.research_topic: Modified laparoscopic needle driver with in-hand needle reorientation capability
teaching_content.motivation: High percentage of needle reorientations required in suturing procedures (33% novice, 38% intermediate, 10% expert, 74% in vivo) increases procedure time by 7.9-21.8 seconds per reorientation
teaching_content.innovation: Standard needle driver modified to allow rolling/rotation of the needle in-hand, inspired by human hand motion, reducing need for tool handoff
key_findings: Modified driver enables completion of stitches that standard driver cannot complete due to workspace/joint limit constraints
User study (13 subjects) shows reduction in hand-off procedures and time lost during needle reorientation
Improved dexterity index and needle tip trajectory optimization
Modified tool allows approximately 50-55 seconds lost time reduction in some user scenarios (vs 105 seconds baseline)
demonstrated_techniques: In-hand needle reorientation mechanism using rolling/rotating gripper
Trajectory planning to avoid joint limits
Comparison of standard vs modified needle driver performance
Biomechanical modeling of needle tip paths
common_errors_addressed: Standard needle drivers reach workspace joint limits during complex stitch trajectories
Necessity of tool handoff/needle reorientation adds time and complexity
Loss of procedural efficiency due to repeated reorientation cycles
teaching_content.target_audience: Surgical education researchers, minimally invasive surgery specialists, surgical tool designers

confidence_rationale: Video content is unambiguously academic/instructional presentation material. Classification as non-performance content is certain based on visual evidence of slides, charts, schematics, and research data rather than live task performance.
